meaning: n.诨号,绰号,昵称vt.给...取绰号,给...起浑名
examples: She professed to hate her nickname.
The vital clue to the killer's identity was his nickname, Peanuts.
His family's Cantonese nickname for him translates as Never Sits Still.
"I was patiently waiting for somebody to give me a nickname that I thought suited me, " he says.
Arriving in Colaba, Lindsay befriends a tour guide of his own, Prabhaker, who gives him the nickname Linbaba.
